prevalence.source: WHO Europe / European pediatric asthma epidemiology syntheses
prevalence.trend: stable; improved control in many systems

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis with variable symptoms; spirometry and FeNO used in many settings; guideline-based assessment per GINA/ERS

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate to high with structured asthma programs; varies by member state

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: High access to spirometry and structured pediatric asthma care in many member states
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ICS-based controller therapy and action plans; school and family education; specialist referral for severe asthma
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Care variability and adherence issues in some groups
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong guideline-based pediatric asthma programs and inhaler access
